Approaching the bookshelf, you only see three books within the finely crafted hardwood furniture, the rather lonely-looking collection kept on the lowest shelf and pressed between the side of the bookcase and what looks like a rock.

Approaching the bookshelf, you only see three books within the finely crafted hardwood furniture, the rather lonely-looking collection kept on the lowest shelf and pressed between the side of the bookcase and what looks like a rock.

Atop it all stands the imperiously gleaming statuette of the Black Knight, the monster champion represented in immaculate detail with her sword plunged into her silver stand, while the figurine's pitch-black box sits behind it like a menacing backdrop.
Take a look at 'Epic of Entropy'.
Take a look at 'Dictionary for the Use of Educated Monsters'.
Take a look at 'Perpetua's Secret {u}Evil{/u} Schemes'.
Take a look at... the rock?
Take a look at her Dark Knight figurine.

EventText.NameOfScene: Take a look at 'Epic of Entropy'.
theScene: {i}Epic of Entropy{/i} looks like a novel, and judging by the stark-black, faux-leather cover and the title in fancy silvery lettering, this particular copy seems to be a limited edition of some sort. You also notice that unlike the other two books on the bookshelf, this one appears in pristine condition, having obviously been treated with immense care.
Behind you, you can almost physically feel Perpetua radiate with enthusiasm and pride.

The jewel of my collection! It's a biography of the Black Knight and all her {i}awesome{/i} deeds in service of the various Demon Queens she worked with! It's super inspirational and {i}super{/i} cool! I wouldn't be the Great Perpetua you know today if I hadn't been enlightened by this glorious tome!
While the slime girl gushes, you grab the book and curiously open a page at random.

{i}'It's over for you now, Black Knight! Your defeat will pave the way for the supremacy of humankind!' The gloating paladin guffawed as she raised her silver sword, ready to strike down the kneeling warrioress, the enchanted blade glinting in the light of the watching stars.{/i}
{i}However, Johanna's expression shifted in an instant from a triumphant sneer to a mask of disbelief as her weapon's deadly arc was abruptly stopped by the Black Knight's iron grip!{/i}
{i}'How can this be!?' The dumbstruck woman ejaculated. 'The Goddess' blessing should have deprived you of your strength!'{/i}
{i}'Foolish human.' The dark knight of destruction replied between gritted teeth. 'As if your puny tricks could do anything against {i}true{/i} power.'{/i}

{i}'Impossible!' She howled as the awesome cosmic might of darkness shattered the pristine steel like brittle wood, her scream turning into a choked sound of pain as the Black Knight's legendary Dark Annihilating Meteor Punch sent her flying, her armored body crashing into the mountainside with a thunderous sound.{/i}
{i}'So long as a single monster stands, I will not be defeated by the likes of you!' The Black Knight yelled, charging forward while the paladin struggled out of the impact crater with a growl{/i}-
Blinking, you raise your eyes from your reading, and realize that Perpetua is right by your shoulder, reading alongside you in giddy engrossment.
I knoooow! You should get your own copy! Then we can talk about it! Oh- And do readings together!♪
I- I'd lend you mine, but I had to save for, like, {i}months{/i} to afford it, and I don't want to lose it or have it get damaged... I know you're my frenemy and all, but-
You assure her that you get it and she beams in relief, all the while clearly making an effort to hold herself back from spoiling the entire story to you as you put the book back in its place.
She seems entirely oblivious to your perplexity and nods happily.
I know, right? It should be obligatory reading for all of Lucidia... and Fertilia, too! It would strike fear and respect into the hearts of humans for sure, ohoho.♪
You decide not to burst her bubble and put the book back in its place while she hums happily.
What do you mean by that, [ThePlayerName]? Of course it's all true! What kind of monster would write lies in a {i}book?{/i}
You feel like part of Perpetua's personality makes more sense as you quietly put the book back in its place under her chiding gaze.
The smallish slime sputters in confused indignation.
She takes the book back from your hands and hugs it protectively, fixing you with a grudgeful stare.
It's epic and emotional and gripping and complex and educative and- and...
She puts the book back in its place and turns away from you with a huff, sludging back a bit to cool down.
She looks like she's actually hurt.

EventText.NameOfScene: Take a look at 'Dictionary for the Use of Educated Monsters'.
theScene: Despite its somewhat pompous title and a preface that goes on for probably too long, the DUEM looks like a fairly standard dictionary... or at least it looks like it used to be one until it was given countless markers, bookmarks, annotations, sticky notes, and even additions under the form of amateurishly glued-on pages, featuring new words such as 'Evilroic', 'Slimeperial', or 'Cherrycious'.
It's important for an evil mastermind to be properly educated, as a trained adventurer like yourself most certainly knows.

It's helped me a lot through my formative years, and even now, I sometimes refer to it or give it upgrades... And once I become Demon Queen, this augmented version can inspire other aspiring villains in turn!
You put the book back while she does a little 'Ohoho.' of celebration, the slime girl having really got it all figured out.

EventText.NameOfScene: Take a look at 'Perpetua's Secret {u}Evil{/u} Schemes'.
theScene: Unlike the other two books, this one looks clearly makeshift, being more of a collection of handwritten loose sheets bound together with thread.
Oh, uh, you're not really supposed to read that... What with them being my secret schemes and all...
But since you're here, I guess you can take a look? Just a quick one, though.
Surprised by her unexpectedly demure reaction, you grab the binder-like volume, taking care not to rip or drop pages, and open it at random to a section titled 'Sleim Vllge Renovation'.

It's actually... fairly sensible? The organization is a bit haphazard, but from what you can tell, the ideas that are exposed and fleshed out across the few pages do hold water, and could actually turn the village into a more prosperous agglomeration... given time... and money... and more willingness from the locals.
Judging by Perpetua's miffed expression, she's well aware of that, and you give her a sympathetic look before turning to another random page, this one titled '[ThePlayerName]'s-
With surprising speed, the book is wrenched out of your hands by a suddenly full-on panicked Perpetua who squeaks out.
You stare back at her in surprise, the slime girl stammering in embarrassment.
Y-Y-You're my frenemy, so you're not supposed to see my intel about you! {i}There!{/i}
She firmly puts it back where it belongs, then quickly recomposes herself like nothing happened.
Something else less super top secret catch your interest?

EventText.NameOfScene: Take a look at... the rock?
theScene: On the bookshelf is a chunk of ordinary-looking crude stone, which seems like it could have come from basically anywhere. In fact, were it not for its position, you might be inclined to assume that Perpetua was just negligent with her cleaning.
Noticing your interest, Perpetua slides closer with a sly and confident smile, holding the stone up for you to look at as she explains.

It's a rock from when I was blown away by the Black Knight's awesome blast of energy!♪ I couldn't get her to sign something... and didn't get to talk to her much... So that was the next best thing, you know? Pretty cool, though, right?
Look at how neatly the stone was cut! It couldn't have been made by anything but the power of one of the mightiest monster girls in existence!
You... can't notice anything in particular. But Perpetua, meanwhile, seems positively enchanted with it, putting it back like she's handling a precious jewel.

EventText.NameOfScene: Take a look at her Dark Knight figurine.
theScene: On the bookcase's top shelf stands a figurine that proudly sits atop a silver base, depicting the Black Knight in carefully immaculate detail, down to every individual joint of her gauntleted hand resting on the pommel of her red and black blade plunged into the stand. Behind the figurine, its 'Darkest Night' box looms formidably, its pitch-black packaging forming an eerie backdrop for the statuette.

Noticing your interest, Perpetua puffs up with pride and sets her hands on her hips as she rises to her full height to speak up, the extra elevation bringing her diminutive half-legged slimy form just barely eye level with the figurine.
I'm sure you remember the {i}amazing{/i} gift the ghost slime sisters gave me as a reward for completing their riddles.♪

And yet, I think the greatest riddle of all was figuring out where to put it... Next to my bed? On a windowsill? Next to the books at the bottom of the shelf? On the kitchen table? ... Or should I instead make room for an {i}exclusive{/i} altar dedicated to the champion of monsterkind?

But in the end, the best, not to mention safest, spot was the top of my bookshelf, dutifully watching over my precious book collection, which is itself guarded by the {i}priceless{/i} artifact I gathered from our momentous first encounter, back when she nearly blew me up.

The slime girl speaks of the subject rather nonchalantly, a gleam of enthusiasm and pride even showing in her eyes, as if getting blasted away by her idol had been an honor and a privilege, though her gaze then turns more distant as she taps her chin in recollection.
Foooor a while, I actually considered trying to put it {i}on{/i} the rock, or atop my books, but I was worried it wouldn't be very stable and it might fall and get damaged.

After all, it {i}is{/i} the 'Darkest Night' limited-edition figurine of the Black Knight! Only {b}fifty{/b} copies were ever made, and she posed {b}herself{/b} for the sculpting! It's even said that she {i}personally{/i} bestowed her dark blessing upon each individual figurine, and that upon moonless nights, these artifacts may grant stygian dreams of eldritch might to their owner, instilling them with a spark of the Black Knight's majesty, spurring them on their path to rise to ever greater depths of power, her oneiric presence bolstering those who strive to support monsterkind!

The sudden tide of babbling hyperfan information hits you like a tsunami, the rapid pace and colorful vocabulary making it hard to absorb it all as she keeps waxing enthusiastic, until she eventually and thankfully winds down with a proud huff, which then unexpectedly turns into a more genuine cheery smile.
But... To be honest, as cool as the figurine may be, I think getting to go on an adventure with you, and getting to know Mycia, Amanita, and Virosa is the real treasure I got.
You make your way back towards the table under Perpetua's awaiting friendly gaze.
Well, we certainly have {i}plenty{/i} to talk about by now, haha.♪

She exclaims cheerfully, scooting over to lift herself up onto one of the chairs by the table, making a series of audible squelching and slapping noises as she makes herself comfortable before gesturing to you to join her with a happy, eager smile.
Perpetua hops off her chair with an audible slapping plop as she hits the wooden flooring, before lazily stretching with a relaxed sigh.
The cherry slime lets out a huff at your pointed question about her house. Her Perpetua-shaped house.
Okay, listen, I didn't make it or plan it. The whole village pitched in with a bunch of stuff on my birthday while I was out, and I came back to this whole... {i}thing.{/i}

I was living basically in a hole in the ground while I tried to make a huge mega-castle... like a skull, but my face, you know? And also with a moat and other evilly aesthetic things... But I'm pretty bad at engineering and stuff- and we don't really have a lot of good stone, so I wasn't getting anywhere and I was starting to think I should give up on the whole idea...
S-So I was kind of taken aback when I saw it... b-but I definitely didn't cry! Shut up!
You blink, having not implied anything, or even spoken at all, the flustered slime rambling on at the memory for a moment longer, then simmering down when she realizes her defensive outburst.
Uh- guh- {i}A-anyways!{/i} I-In the end, it's way less threatening and doom-secret-lair-y than I wanted it to be...
But it {i}is{/i} really cozy! It's warm all year, I can keep all my stuff without it getting dirty, the bed is slime-proof and comfy... it's {b}great{/b}!
She looks around her home with a warm smile before letting out a heavy sigh and staring at the table, broodingly tracing shapes on it with a finger.

I hope some day the whole village can have houses like this... Silver keeps trying to save up money for improvements... and she and the whole town splurged a bunch of it on me... They didn't even make a big deal of it or anything, it was just... {i}natural{/i} for them, you know?

She sighs with undisguised fondness, squeezing her fist in a silent show of determination, and it looks like it's all she has to say on the subject, but then, a reminiscence flickers across her expression as an afterthought leaves her pouting a bit forlornly.
...Also I gave up on the moat when I realized it'd kill all my cherry trees if I wanted to do it right... or at least that it would be a massive pain to uproot them, move them, and replant them...
She reaches forward and snags a cherry from the bowl in the center which she happily tosses into her mouth, the swallowed fruit soon dissolving inside her while she waits for the next topic.
Perpetua does a spit take, except instead of a drink, she sputters some of her own slime before lifting herself up on the table with her arms in squeaky panic.

Even before you finish that sentence, she's already huffily settling back into her wooden chair with grumpily crossed arms, glaring off at a window. Following her gaze, you could swear that you saw a blue shape quickly moving away...
{i}One of the slimes did.{/i} Of {i}course{/i} they did.

Listen, that's my old name. These slimebrains think it's cute, but it's just {i}lame{/i}. My {i}new{/i} and {i}true{/i} villain name is {i}Per{/i}petua, an unending gooey tide of villainous slimy doom!
She raises her head smugly, apparently very proud with the word, and concludes firmly.
So just keep calling me Perpetua.
L-Let's just talk about something else, already!
The cherry slime finishes with a definitive huff, tightening her crossed arms with a small gooey squelch, while you could swear you can hear giggling outside...
She gives you an affirming and prideful nod at your understanding, leaning back in her seat.
As you think about what else you could talk about, you could swear you can hear a giggly voice say 'Petua.♪' outside.
Perpetua's expression immediately lights up at the mention, the slime's voice getting unusually high-pitched as she immediately starts spazzing.

{i}Oh my gosh,{/i} the Black Knight is the {i}coolest{/i}!♪ She's the Demon Queen's right-hand woman and one of the most powerful monsters {i}ever{/i}!♪ EEEEE!♪♪♪ I can't believe I got to see her in action up close! Remember how she blew me up from that lift!? That was {i}awesome!{/i} I'm never gonna forget that!♪ I kept some of the rubble as a memento, you know!

Perpetua gushes fangirlishly, bouncing excitedly in her seat at the memory of your encounter with the Black Knight at the Mountain lift, the gooey girl looking like she's going to shoot up from her chair from all that excitement before a sudden thought eventually makes her simmer down, her starstruck expression giving way to a slightly pouty huff.
Never did get that autograph, though...
Then again, I didn't actually have my book on me...

She says with a hint of bashfulness as she looks over to her mostly empty bookshelf, where you notice a stark black book in pristine condition with silvery text reading 'Epic of Entropy', alongside another, much more worn book reading 'Dictionary for the Use of Educated Monsters', and a third volume which, on closer sight, actually looks more like a scrapbook titled 'Perpetua's Secret {u}Evil{/u} Schemes'.
Noticing where your attention is directed, a smile of giddy enthusiasm returns on Perpetua's face.

Cool edition, huh? 'Epic of Entropy' is a retelling of all the Black Knight's adventures over the centuries: a nameless dark villainess who's always at the side of the Demon Queen, wielding the powers of cosmic entropy to terrorize those who oppose monsters while bringing hope and prosperity to monsterkind!♪ Pushing through the monotony of an endless cycle of status quo until happiness can be found!

She keeps going on at such a fast pace and with such sparklingly radiant joy that you have neither the heart nor the opportunity to interrupt her, the small gooey monster vibrating excitedly as she mimics the Black Knight's low, imperious voice and gestures animatedly, as if 'wielding powers of cosmic entropy'.
I, {i}of course{/i}, have perfectly emulated everything I read in there, flawlessly incorporating it into my {i}peerlessly evil{/i} lifestyle.
Unexpectedly, however, she abruptly deflates, settling back down into her chair with a miffed look, squirming in embarrassment.
You know... aside from the... overwhelmingly awesome powers...
Aren't you kind of short for a slime?
Huh? All those weirdos in town? There's not much to say about them...
They're a buncha overly affectionate airheads. Most of 'em, at least. Can be {i}really{/i} annoying, too...
Though at least, a few of them like Silver get what I'm trying to accomplish!♪
She leans onto the table with her elbows and huffs, blowing thoughtfully at a strand of her gooey 'hair' that wobbles slightly as she rests her cheeks in her slimy palms.

Meanwhile, most of 'em are just kinda... content with this crummy village we have... They just don't have any {i}ambition{/i}... And it doesn't help that they're, well, slimes. It hurts to say, but we're just bottom-of-the-barrel monsters. Aside from a few exceptions, we never make it past Level 5 at best, and adventurers don't even pay attention to us... except for some weirdos who have a thing for it...

As for those namby-pambies, they're happy to just live a simple life with ramshackle houses and blueberries on the lakeside, playing all day, sleeping all night... Like- It's {i}nice{/i}, but it's so... boring... repetitive... {i}unfulfilling{/i}. Like the same day plays out on repeat over and {i}over{/i}... Not to mention how paltry our 'village' is...
{i}Especially after seeing that massive Capital!{/i} It was {i}so{/i} impressive, dangit, there's absolutely no comparison! It's like night and day! Like a blue slime versus the Black Knight!
I mean- You saw that place, too. It's {i}amazing{/i}... Though I don't think anyone here would want something like that- Even Silver. I know {i}I{/i} don't...
But that's just more proof I need to help drag those slimebrains into a better future as the ultimate slime villain! Ohohoho!♪
Hm? You mean me? Well... Honestly, I'm not {i}much{/i} different from a blue slime.

Slimes are kind of a 'you are what you eat' deal, hahaha! I eat a lot of cherries, so I became red, while the others mostly stick to blueberries and water... Though really, when all is said and done, it doesn't really change anything aside from what we look and taste like.
Well- I mean- There {i}might{/i} be some kind of difference- But heck if I know. I'm not a slimentist.
She shrugs and looks up thoughtfully while lazily tracing the rim of the wooden bowl in the center of the table with a finger, trying to see if she has anything else to mention.
...I think green ones mostly eat apples and some kinds of rocks? Which makes them all bubbly and stuff?
She again gives a 'heck if I know' shrug and leans back into her chair, having plainly said all she had to say on the topic.

EventText.NameOfScene: Aren't you kind of short for a slime?
theScene: Perpetua sputters as you bring up the height difference between her and the other slimes you've encountered thus far. She's certainly smaller than even the blue ones, which are not very tall to begin with...

Y-You listen here!- I-I'm not {i}short!{/i} It's just hard to keep my legs together! I-I don't have a lot of slime, you know! B-But I'll sort that out when I get stronger! I'll be {i}bigger{/i} than all of the village's slimes combined! Just like with that weird demon capsule thingy!
She lets out a huff and pouts, shooting you a reproachful glare then looking off to the side in thought.

Thinking of it, that purple slime lady in the Capital managed to keep full legs and even feet sorta things without sliding around on a goop puddle... Wonder how she did that... Never seen a slime like that...
While she taps her chin with little gooey 'plip' noises, you look off to the house's window and notice a blue slime smushed against the pane with a smile on her face.
Wh-guh- He'snotmyboyfriend! When did you even get here!? Get outta here, ya weirdo!!!

The small slime woman flails as she hops from her chair to vengefully shoo away her spying brethren, who scoots away with a completely undaunted giggle, leaving Perpetua to fume and hop back into her chair with a wet slap and a grumbly sigh.
I swear, one day, I'll need to add 'KEEP OUT' signs or something...
Huh, what kind of question is that? Don't you humans learn anything?♪ Mitsosi- Mistosi- Mitos- ... Mitioiuousious, duh.
She says confidently while completely butchering whatever word she was trying to say.
After we eat enough food, we split off into a new slime... But like, it's a LOT of food. Doesn't happen more than once every few years, tops.
Then the new slime is, like, a sorta-copy of the original? But not really? It keeps a bunch of your know-how and stuff, but she's her own slime...
Kinda surprised it's never happened to me yet. Having my own minion army of basically myself would be really useful! Perpetua brains, power, and evilness times plenty, ohohoho!
An apparently painful memory then returns to her, the slime girl wincing with a pout of distaste.
I... actually tried to make it happen, once, eating {i}so many{/i} cherries, but all I did was make myself sick, and almost getting disgusted with cherries... can you imagine!?
Uh- Wh-What about it? We're kind of on break though...
Why did you leave the... Sliem Villge?
Were you okay in the caverns?
So about what happened in the Capital...

EventText.NameOfScene: Why did you leave the... Sliem Villge?
theScene: She retorts in a serious corrective tone, raising a professorial finger before continuing.

And to answer the question, I left to become the {i}ultimate{/i} villain and eventually the Demon Queen, of course! Shouldn't you know that, by now, silly [ThePlayerName]? I'll show all those {i}doubters{/i} that a slime can be Demon Queen........ Eventually.
She states plainly, excitedly, and matter of factly, waving her arm decisively.
Alright, but why the whole 'Demon Queen' project?
The question actually takes her aback for a bit, and after taking a second to recollect herself, she goes on more soberly, though still with very perpetuesque certainty.

Power, fame, and to make Sleim Vllge less of... a dump? I mean, despite how pretty and naturey it is, our {i}literal{/i} corner of the woods' standard of living is pretty low overall. And with how complacent most slimes are... this place gets {i}so{/i} boring and monotonous. And since we have basically nothing to export except a pittance of blueberries and fish, it takes us forever to save up for anything...
So that's why I decided to kick things into gear and left to enact my {i}evil{/i} schemes.♪
She laughs contentedly, rubbing her hands together with a scheming expression.

Once I'm a successful mega villain, I'll make Sleim Vllge into a {i}real{/i} paradise for slimes.♪ The new Jewel of Lucidia! The Pride of Monsterkind! A place even humans will think about with fear and awe! Ohohohoho!
Perpetua finishes with a peal of confident laughter that shows off her obvious pride in her intentions of villainy.

Isn't it obvious? If human champions are heroes, then a monster girl like I needs to be a villain! It's a yin and yang thing, you know? In that state of opposition, I can push myself to ever greater heights and eventually become {i}Demon Queen{/i}!♪
But it's turned out to be, huh... a lot harder than I thought it would be...

When you're a slime, your level cap isn't very high, and from what the blue slimes told me, though most adventurers are perverts who keep surrendering to whatever monster they encounter, some of them are {i}really{/i} strong! Like- Well, like you...

And since the whole... 'Demonic Convergence' thing, like that Voltlin lady called it, I've been... kind of thinking really hard on some things, 'cause- I... I don't know... When {i}that{/i} happened, I was... well, evil- A villain, like I wanted... But it felt... gross, and wrong... I had {i}evil{/i} thoughts. Like {i}actually{/i} evil, and that's not the kind of evilness I want...
She shudders at the memory, but then shakes her head off the depressing, complex considerations and gives you a cheerful smile.
Anyway, the most important thing is that being a villain is {i}fun{/i}!♪ Our rivalry has been a total blast!♪

So I'll figure something out to keep moving forward... somehow. I won't just give up and spend my days splashing around water, eating fruit, and then going to bed to do it all over again the next day.
'I'll let you know if I come upon something.'
Wha- I-I don't need your support- You're my rival and stuff!?
Yeah, yeah... What are you, my mom?
Don't worry, I'll just keep working at it until I get there! Bit by bit.♪
She crosses her arms and scoffs with an air of challenge.
Don't care. It's better I keep trying and failing than just giving up... Honestly, I'm kind of {i}insulted{/i} you'd even suggest resigning myself to a boring life of sliming around...
A thought seems to spark in her mind at the notion, and she strikes a confident pose as she lets out a bout of evil laughter.
Were you trying to discourage me? Fiendish, but futile! You're not going to get rid of me that easily, [ThePlayerName]. {i}Ohohoho!{/i}

EventText.NameOfScene: 'I'll let you know if I come upon something.'
theScene: You're my {i}rival{/i}, you're not supposed to help me. It's like- cheating and wrong and stuff!
The great Perpetua will become the ultimate villainess her own way!♪ Just you watch!
She puffs up pridefully at the reminiscence, plainly glad for the callback question.

Oho, how nice and astute of you to bring it up, [ThePlayerName]! It was the first step of my master plan to become a {i}true{/i} villain. Strike fear into that puny human settlement to feed my infamy and throw the gauntlet of challenge, destroying any foolish humans sent to stop me! ...
I may have gotten a {i}little{/i} bit ahead of myself, to be honest. I definitely wasn't that strong back then... And I made it up with a lot of... uh... bluster...

Also I heard on the harpy grapevine that the ladies who run the Guild are apparently super powerful veteran adventurers who've defeated {i}dragons{/i}!? Th-That's scary! I thought they'd just be a bunch of harmless pen-pushers!
There's obvious shame in her panicked face as she recollects the events before swallowing her embarrassment, perking up and smiling at you happily.

But though it was kind of a mess, I {i}did{/i} get a rival out of it! Which pushed me to get even stronger, just like {i}Epic of Entropy{/i} said! I even got to see all kinds of new- and in some cases, terrible, places! It was {i}cool{/i}!
The stars in her eyes wink out as she remembers something and slumps back into her chair ruefully.

I couldn't wait to tell the others about it all, but I kinda forgot how they can be. They were all 'Wow.' and 'Oooh.', but they're not exactly hard to impress, and even though I know they meant it, it quickly got tiresome when they kept congratulating me... and trying to pat my head even though they know I hate that!!!

EventText.NameOfScene: Were you okay in the caverns?
theScene: The diminutive slime girl immediately becomes even smaller, shrinking a few inches as she retreats into her own slime at the memory.
I don't wanna talk about it!!!
She sputters before proceeding to talk about it anyways, her thoughts flowing out of her mouth in a rambling stream.

It was dark! Cold! Full of those stupid Salarisi bullies! There was some horrific crunch in this one big cave, and a spooky shape with one glowing silvery eye! And I don't know how long I was even in that creepy cave network! Then Amy wouldn't stop chasing me and licking me! I'm not going back there, not for a million eros!

Worried that she's actually going to lose her mind, you reach out to comfortingly pat her back, the slime woman letting out a steady exhale and hastily picking up a cherry from the bowl on the table to calm her nerves after her outburst before perking up again.

Though I gotta say that those hellhound ladies... Well, they spooked the core out of me at first, but once we were past that, they turned out to be really nice!- Sure, the blue one kept scoffing, the snakey one just looked confused, and the green one looked at me weird... probably because she sensed that I was serious competition! But the red one was very friendly! Really warm hugs. Great temp minions.
She nods confidently, certain of 'her' minions' efforts against you.

EventText.NameOfScene: So about what happened in the Capital...
theScene: Having quite clearly been prepared for that avenue of inquiry, Perpetua dips her head.
Uh... Yeah... That was a whole... {i}thing.{/i}
She heavily sighs and reaches for the bowl in the center of the table to grab a cherry to devour, giving you a weak smile at the scathing remembrance.
Thanks- uh, for stopping me and stuff... I promise I won't ever again take suspicious gifts from people in cloaks for power and stuff... Um...
She doesn't need more prompting to keep going, continuing to munch on cherries while removing the stems to make little shapes with them on the table.

Being like that was awful... Like... I was trapped in my own body, watching what I was doing, like some kind of weird dream... Everything except being a villain and making you fall fo- {i}I mean defeating you{/i} just- {i}didn't matter{/i}... It felt... incredibly wrong... and gross... More like a nightmare than a dream, really...
She tries to get a grip on herself and chase away the sulking drooping of her gooey form at the memory, giving you a warm smile.
